Control_Objective: To provide management direction and support for information security in accordance with business requirements
Real_World_Scenario: QuickMart must have a written Information Security Policy approved by management, communicated to all staff, and reviewed annually
Risk_Addressed: Lack of policy direction leads to inconsistent security practices and no accountability
Policy_Gap_Identified: Sample ISMS policy reviewed lacked a defined review cycle and management sign-off — policy existed but had no version control or approval date

Control_Objective: Establish clear roles and responsibilities for information security across the organisation
Real_World_Scenario: QuickMart assigns the IT owner as the Data Protection Officer; delivery staff are trained on what customer data they can access and what they cannot
Risk_Addressed: Without defined roles, no one is accountable when a data breach occurs
Policy_Gap_Identified: Policy document did not define who is responsible for enforcing controls — no RACI or role matrix present

Control_Objective: Background verification checks on all candidates should be carried out prior to joining
Real_World_Scenario: QuickMart performs basic identity verification for delivery partners and part-time staff before granting access to the ordering platform
Risk_Addressed: Unscreened staff with access to customer PII increases risk of insider threat
Policy_Gap_Identified: No mention of pre-employment screening in the reviewed sample policy

Control_Objective: Assets associated with information and information processing facilities shall be identified and an inventory maintained
Real_World_Scenario: QuickMart maintains a documented register of all information assets: customer database, payment gateway, inventory system, CCTV, employee records, email accounts
Risk_Addressed: Without asset inventory, the organisation cannot protect what it does not know it has
Policy_Gap_Identified: Reviewed sample ISMS policy contained no asset inventory process or asset register template — critical omission for ISO 27001 compliance

Control_Objective: Information shall be classified in terms of legal requirements
Applicable_To_QuickMart: and sensitivity to unauthorised disclosure or modification
Policy_Gap_Identified: QuickMart classifies customer PII and payment card data as Confidential, operational data as Internal, and public promotions as Public
Non_Conformity_Ref: Unclassified information may be handled, stored, or transmitted without adequate protection
column10: Sample policy had no data classification scheme or handling instructions by classification level

Control_Objective: An access control policy shall be established
Risk_Addressed: and reviewed based on business and information security requirements
Applicable_To_QuickMart: QuickMart restricts system access by role: only the store manager can access HR records; delivery staff only access order status — no shared credentials permitted
Policy_Gap_Identified: Unrestricted access increases the risk of unauthorised disclosure and insider misuse
column9: Policy reviewed stated 'access is role-based' but provided no specifics on who approves access, how it is granted, or how exceptions are handled

Control_Objective: Formal user registration and de-registration process shall be implemented for granting and revoking access
Real_World_Scenario: When a delivery partner leaves QuickMart, their system credentials are revoked within 24 hours and access logs are reviewed
Risk_Addressed: Failure to remove access for departed users is a leading cause of insider data breach
Policy_Gap_Identified: No user on-boarding or off-boarding procedure defined in the sample policy reviewed — access review cycle entirely absent

Control_Objective: Access to systems and applications shall be restricted in line with the access control policy
Real_World_Scenario: QuickMart requires unique username and strong password for each staff login to the ordering platform; MFA enabled for admin accounts
Risk_Addressed: Shared or weak credentials allow unauthorised access to sensitive systems
Policy_Gap_Identified: Sample policy did not specify password complexity requirements or MFA mandates

Control_Name: Policy on the Use of Cryptographic Controls
Control_Objective: Establish and implement a policy on the use of cryptographic controls for protection of information
Real_World_Scenario: QuickMart ensures all customer payment data is encrypted in transit (TLS 1.2+) and at rest (AES-256) on the database server
Risk_Addressed: Unencrypted payment data in transit is directly exploitable via man-in-the-middle attacks
Policy_Gap_Identified: Reviewed policy made no mention of encryption standards or key management procedures

Control_Objective: Security perimeters shall be defined and used to protect areas that contain sensitive information or critical information processing facilities
Real_World_Scenario: QuickMart restricts physical access to server room and office area: keycard access only, CCTV monitoring at entry points, no tailgating policy
Risk_Addressed: Unrestricted physical access allows theft of hardware, direct data extraction, or sabotage
Policy_Gap_Identified: Sample policy did not address physical access controls or visitor management

Risk_Addressed: and made available to all users who need them
Applicable_To_QuickMart: QuickMart documents daily system backup procedures, incident escalation steps, and server maintenance schedules in a shared internal wiki
Policy_Gap_Identified: Undocumented operations lead to inconsistency, errors, and inability to recover from incidents
column9: Policy reviewed referenced operational procedures but provided no procedure templates or links

Risk_Addressed: and system images shall be taken and tested regularly
Applicable_To_QuickMart: QuickMart performs daily automated backups of the customer database and inventory system to an off-site cloud location; backup restoration is tested quarterly
Policy_Gap_Identified: Without tested backups, ransomware or hardware failure results in permanent data loss
column9: No backup frequency, retention period, or restoration testing requirement specified in the sample policy

Applicable_To_QuickMart: and information security events shall be produced
column9: QuickMart logs all admin login events (Windows Event ID 4624/4625), failed access attempts, and payment gateway transactions; logs retained for 90 days
column10: Without logging, security incidents cannot be detected, investigated, or evidenced for compliance
column12: Sample policy did not define what events must be logged, log retention duration, or who is responsible for log review

Control_Objective: Responsibilities and procedures shall be established to ensure a quick
Risk_Addressed: and orderly response to information security incidents
Applicable_To_QuickMart: QuickMart has a defined incident response procedure: detect, contain, eradicate, recover, report; all incidents logged with severity classification and timeline
Policy_Gap_Identified: Without an incident response process, breaches go uncontrolled and regulatory notification deadlines are missed
column9: No incident reporting procedure or escalation path defined in the reviewed sample policy — a critical gap for regulatory compliance

Control_Name: Compliance with Legal and Contractual Requirements
Applicable_To_QuickMart: and contractual requirements shall be identified
column9: QuickMart maintains a compliance register identifying applicable regulations (IT Act 2000, DPDP Act 2023, PCI-DSS for payment processing) and reviews it annually
column10: Non-compliance with data protection laws exposes the organisation to regulatory fines and reputational damage
column12: Sample policy contained no legal compliance register or reference to applicable Indian data protection regulations
